steps.title: Use the LNH prediction
steps.content: The LNH predicts an inverse relationship between exotericity and grammatical complexity. Since fusion and informativity are positive indices of complexity, evidence against the LNH should show those indices rising with exotericity.

steps.title: Look for the opposite pattern
steps.content: Choice A does not compare exotericity with the indices, Choice B supports the predicted inverse relationship, and Choice D compares complexity with its own indices. The remaining choice gives positive relationships with exotericity. The answer is C.
